evals.prompt: I want to train a model with GRPO on GSM8K math problems. Give me a complete script.
evals.expected_output: Runnable RL script using Gsm8kDatasetBuilder with appropriate hyperparameters
expectations: Uses Gsm8kDatasetBuilder from tinker_cookbook.recipes.math_rl.math_env
Uses train.Config from tinker_cookbook.rl.train
Sets group_size (typically 4-16) and batch_size
Uses a learning rate in the 1e-5 to 4e-5 range (appropriate for RL)
Uses model_info.get_recommended_renderer_name() for renderer
The script is complete and runnable
Explains that advantages are centered within each group (why group_size matters)
Mentions available loss functions (importance_sampling as default, ppo, cispo, dro)
Warns about sampler desync — must create new SamplingClient after weight saves

evals.prompt: I have a custom task where the model answers trivia questions. How do I create a custom RL environment for this?
evals.expected_output: ProblemEnv subclass with get_question, check_answer, check_format, get_reference_answer methods
expectations: Subclasses ProblemEnv from tinker_cookbook.rl.problem_env
Implements get_question(), check_answer(), check_format(), and get_reference_answer()
Shows how to wrap it with ProblemGroupBuilder
Creates an RLDatasetBuilder that produces batches of the custom environment
Mentions that Env objects are single-use (no reset)
Explains the reward formula: format_coef * (check_format - 1) + check_answer
Uses @chz.chz decorator on the RLDatasetBuilder for config serialization

evals.prompt: I need to train a model to use a search tool in a multi-turn conversation. How do I set up the environment?
evals.expected_output: MessageEnv subclass with initial_observation and step methods, wrapped with EnvFromMessageEnv
expectations: Uses MessageEnv from tinker_cookbook.rl.message_env
Implements initial_observation() returning list[Message] and step() returning MessageStepResult
Wraps with EnvFromMessageEnv to bridge to the token-level Env interface
Sets max_trajectory_tokens to limit context growth
Mentions AsyncConfig for expensive multi-turn environments
Suggests kl_penalty_coef=0.0 for tool-use exploration
Mentions cleanup() method on EnvGroupBuilder for releasing resources

evals.prompt: I have an RL training loop that samples rollouts, scores them with a reward model, and trains. How do I write it to maximize throughput?
evals.expected_output: Async RL loop that overlaps rollouts with training and evaluates concurrently
expectations: Uses forward_backward_async and optim_step_async submitted back-to-back before awaiting
Overlaps next batch rollouts with current training step (not sequential)
Evaluates or scores rollouts concurrently — NOT in a sequential for loop
Creates a new SamplingClient after weight saves for subsequent rollouts
Mentions AsyncConfig for overlapping sampling and training when env execution is slow
Does not write sequential .result() chains that serialize GPU work
